To which Public Authority can I file a r: The RTI Online Portal of the Government of India is a platform for filing Right to Information (RTI) applications electronically to various Central Government public authorities in India. You can find a list of these public authorities on the portal. To file a request, you need to select the appropriate public authority from the list based on the subject matter of your request. Once you have identified the public authority, you can then proceed to file your application through the portal. It is important to note that not all public authorities may be listed on the portal, and for those that are not, you may still file your application in the traditional paper format.
How do I make the payment for RTI fee?:
To make a payment for RTI (Right to Information) fees through the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India, follow these steps:

1. Log in to the RTI Online Portal using your registered account or create a new account if you don't have one.
2. Go to the "New Application" tab and fill in the necessary details for your RTI application.
3. After filling in the details, you will be directed to the fee payment page.
4. Choose the mode of payment - Online payment through credit/debit card, net banking, or UPI.
5. Enter the payment details and complete the transaction.
6. Once the payment is successful, you will receive a confirmation message and a receipt number.
7. Submit your RTI application with the receipt number and other required documents.

What happens if I select a wrong Public: If you select a wrong Public Authority while using the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India, it may result in your application being rejected or delayed. Each Public Authority has its own unique identification number and specific email address for receiving RTI applications. Submitting an application to the wrong Public Authority may lead to unnecessary delays or even rejection of your application. To avoid this, it is important to carefully check the details of the Public Authority before submitting your application. If you are unsure about which Public Authority to approach, you can contact the Central Public Information Officer (CPIO) or the First Appellate Authority (FAA) of the concerned Public Authority for clarification.
Do I get any receipt for online filing o: Yes, after you submit your RTI application through the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India, you will receive an acknowledgement receipt with a unique application number. This receipt will be sent to the email address and mobile number provided by you during the application process. You can use this application number to check the status of your application on the portal. Additionally, you may also receive a hard copy of the acknowledgement receipt if you have opted for the 'Speed Post' delivery option while filing your application.
What should I do if I forgot my login cr:
If you have forgotten your login credentials for the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India, you can follow these steps to retrieve or reset them:

1. Go to the RTI Online Portal website: Visit the official RTI Online Portal website at https://rtionline.gov.in/

2. Forgot Password: Click on the "Forgot Password" link available on the login page.

3. Enter your registered email ID: Enter the email ID associated with your RTI Online Portal account and click on the "Submit" button.

4. Verify Email ID: You will receive an email on your registered email ID with a verification link. Click on the link to verify your email ID.

5. Reset Password: After verifying your email ID, you will be redirected to the password reset page. Enter a new password and confirm it by entering it again.

Can I file RTI application for state pub:
Yes, you can file RTI applications for state public authorities in India through the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India. However, not all state public authorities are part of the portal, so it's important to check if the specific authority you're trying to file an application against is included. You can do this by searching for the authority in the portal's "Search for Information" section or by contacting the concerned State RTI Cell for clarification.

To file an application, you'll need to create a user account on the portal if you don't already have one, and then follow the steps to create a new application. Make sure to provide all necessary details, including your contact information, the name and address of the public authority, and a clear description of the information you're seeking. Once your application is submitted, you'll receive a unique application number, which you can use to track the status of your request.
How much time does RTI request retain at: The RTI (Right to Information) requests and their corresponding responses maintained on the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India are retained for a period of five years from the date of disposal of the request. After this period, they are automatically deleted from the system. However, it is important to note that the physical records and documents related to an RTI request may still be retained by the public authority concerned as per their record retention policies.
Why is OTP feature introduced in view st: The One-Time Password (OTP) feature in the RTI Online Portal of the Government of India has been introduced as an additional security measure to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive information. When you check the status of an RTI application, the system may require you to enter an OTP that is sent to your registered mobile number to verify your identity. This helps ensure that only the person who originally submitted the application has access to the information related to it. This feature enhances the security and confidentiality of the information exchanged between the applicant and the public authority.
What are the reasons for RTI payment fai:
There could be several reasons why a payment might fail when using the RTI (Right to Information) portal of the Government of India. Here are some common reasons:

1. Insufficient funds: If you don't have enough balance in your bank account, the payment may fail.
2. Incorrect payment details: Ensure that you enter the correct bank account number, IFSC code, and other payment details.
3. Network issues: If there are network connectivity issues while making the payment, it may fail.
4. Technical glitches: Sometimes, there might be technical issues with the RTI portal or your browser, causing the payment to fail.
5. Incorrect application form: Make sure that you have filled out the RTI application form correctly and have attached all necessary documents, if required.
6. Payment gateway issues: If there are issues with the payment gateway, the payment may fail. In such cases, you may need to try
Do I need to make any payment for filing: The RTI Online Portal of the Government of India does not charge any fee for filing an appeal. However, there may be certain circumstances where you are required to pay a fee for obtaining information under the Right to Information Act, 2005. This fee is payable at the time of filing the original RTI application and not at the time of filing an appeal. Please note that the fee structure varies depending on the category of information sought and the applicability of certain exemptions. For more information, you may refer to the RTI Rules and Guidelines or contact the concerned Public Information Officer or Appellate Authority.
